title: moniera procumbens var. normalis
intro: Moniera procumbens var. normalis, also known as Indian pennywort or bashak, is a type of flowering plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective effects. It is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving cognitive function, and supporting immune system health. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Moniera procumbens var. normalis plant are typically used to make teas, capsules, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
